title: Aspalathus horizontalis
taxonomy: Aspalathus horizontalis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Aspalathus, which is a group of plants native to South Africa. The plant is commonly known as the honeybush tea tree due to its edible flowers and leaves used for making tea. Further classification includes its being a part of the subfamily Faboideae and the tribe Crotalarieae. As a member of the Fabaceae family, it is also related to other legume plants.

The plant's leaves and stems have been used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. It is also valued for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
